top_signals.title: Middle East Oil Collapses as Contango Returns on Supply Hope
top_signals.summary: Middle Eastern crude markets have collapsed, as the US-Iran agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz spurs optimism around an increase in flows from the oil-rich region.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: An Oil Deal That Ended a War Nobody Won
top_signals.summary: What happened today is best described, despite much more dramatic categorizations coming out of Tehran and Washington, is a 60-day ceasefire extension, now reportedly signed by both parties, that reopens the Strait of Hormuz, while the original alleged reason for going to war, the nuclear weapons issue, stays open on the negotiating table. Both sides are buying time in order to get the oil flowing through Hormuz.  So, today’s deal is an oil deal, first and foremost. It’s not a victory for anyone. It’s just bringing things…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: India files charges against 8 defendants in alleged $20 million Coinbase spoofing scam
top_signals.summary: India's Directorate of Enforcement charged Chirag Tomar and associates over an alleged $20 million Coinbase spoofing scheme.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Magnitude 5.5 quake jolts Tokyo area
top_signals.summary: The quake, measuring a lower 5 on Japan's seismic intensity scale, struck southern Ibaraki Prefecture at 7:46 p.m. There was no threat of a tsunami.
